title: Introductory Python Foundations

topics.title: Variables
topics.summary: Naming values and updating them through simple assignments.

topics.title: Data Types
topics.summary: Recognizing strings, integers, floats, booleans, and basic conversion patterns.

topics.title: Conditionals
topics.summary: Using if, elif, and else to branch on simple decisions.

topics.title: Loops
topics.summary: Repeating work with for and while loops, including simple tracing and stopping conditions.

topics.title: Functions
topics.summary: Defining reusable blocks, passing inputs, and returning values.

topics.title: Lists
topics.summary: Creating ordered collections and iterating through list items.

topics.title: Dictionaries
topics.summary: Using key-value mappings for simple lookup tasks.

topics.title: Debugging Basics
topics.summary: Reading error messages, checking assumptions, and tracing small examples.

topics.title: Mini-Project Review
topics.summary: Combining the earlier topics into a short review activity with reflection prompts.

quiz_prompt_templates.template: Present a short loop and ask the learner to predict the printed output and explain one iteration step.
Show a small function with one missing or incorrect line and ask the learner to repair it using plain-language reasoning.
Give a small scenario and ask whether a list or dictionary is the better fit, with a short explanation.
Provide a short error message and ask the learner to identify the likely cause and the next local check.

revision_prompts: Explain a loop in your own words and give one example of when it should stop.
Describe the difference between printing a value and returning a value from a function.
Write one sentence that explains when a dictionary is more useful than a list.
List two debugging checks you can do before changing the code.
Summarize today's topic in plain language without using formal jargon.
